today_in_history_posts.title: Bitcoin mail list needs an explicit moderation policy
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Maxim Orlovsky criticized unclear moderation and potential censorship on the bitcoin-dev mail list.
- Orlovsky suggested creating a non-censored list and replacing current moderator Bryan Bishop.
- He provided evidence of bias and abuse of power by Bishop in his role as moderator.

today_in_history_posts.title: Liquidity griefing for 0-conf dual-funded txs
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Liquidity griefing in dual-funded transactions locks UTXOs, making them unusable.  - Proposed solution suggests not locking UTXOs, allowing reuse if a node goes silent.  - "Soft lock" on UTXOs could prevent accidental double-spending in 0-conf channels.

today_in_history_posts.title: FE'd Up Covenants
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The paper details an innovative method for integrating covenants into the Bitcoin protocol.
- It utilizes Functional Encryption and Zero Knowledge Proofs to enhance Bitcoin’s security and functionality.
- This approach allows for more sophisticated contract control without compromising privacy.
